data_notes: Used combined_rollups.json as authoritative for last-7 and last-28 load totals (distance, moving time, elevation, training load).
Last 7 days have 0 sleep days with data, so last-7 sleep/HRV/RHR means are unknown (null in signals) and were not inferred.

No personal biometrics (e.g., lactate threshold HR) were available, so intensity guidance is based on session type, HR presence in activities, and conservative distribution rather than HR-zone prescriptions.
Weekly plan moving time is ~9.5 h, staying under the max ramp constraint (<= 12.46 h) relative to last 7 days (11.324 h).
Readiness status was set from deterministic readiness_and_risk_forecast.json.
Guardrails: enforced ramp rate (max_ramp_pct=10.0%). last7_hours=11.324, allowed_hours=12.456, planned_hours=9.500.

meta.style: trail+endurance (bike-heavy) with conservative load due to elevated recent training load and limited recovery telemetry

days.purpose: Absorb the elevated recent load while keeping frequency and leg circulation.
days.target_intensity: Easy conversational aerobic throughout; keep pressure off the pedals (no sustained efforts).
days.terrain: Flat to rolling; avoid long climbs today.
days.title: Easy aerobic spin + cadence focus
days.workout: 75 min easy Ride. Include 6 x 20s high-cadence spin-ups (very light resistance) with full easy riding between.

days.purpose: Maintain trail specificity and durability without stacking intensity after an elevated-load week.
days.target_intensity: Easy all day; hike the steeper climbs to keep effort smooth and sustainable.
days.terrain: Trail with moderate climbing; aim ~700–1000 m vertical (stay below last week’s big-vert day feel).
days.title: Long easy trail (run/hike) with controlled vertical
days.workout: 140 min trail run/hike. Keep downhills controlled. Optional: last 10 min very easy if legs feel beat up.

days.purpose: Guarantee at least one true down day to reduce overreach risk given elevated recent load and missing recovery data.
days.title: Rest day (full off) + mobility
days.workout: OFF. 15–25 min gentle mobility (hips/ankles/T-spine) + light walk only if you feel stiff.

days.purpose: One focused stimulus this week to maintain fitness while keeping total hard days low.
days.target_intensity: Hard but repeatable; stop the set if HR/legs drift excessively. Keep it controlled—not a test.
days.terrain: Trainer or steady road segment with minimal interruptions.
days.title: Quality day: bike intervals (controlled)
days.workout: Warm-up 15 min easy. Main set: 5 x 4 min hard / 3 min easy. Cool-down 10–15 min easy.

days.purpose: Consolidate the quality day while building low-intensity volume safely.
days.target_intensity: Comfortably easy aerobic; keep it smooth after yesterday’s quality.
days.title: Easy aerobic ride (endurance reset)
days.workout: 90 min easy Ride. Keep cadence comfortable; no structured efforts.

days.purpose: Maintain running economy and add musculoskeletal resilience without adding another hard cardiovascular day.
days.target_intensity: Run easy; strength controlled (leave 2–3 reps in reserve).
days.terrain: Flat to gentle trail/road for run; strength at home/gym.
days.title: Short easy run + strength (trail durability)
days.workout: Easy run 45 min (keep it relaxed). Then 25–30 min strength: split squats, RDLs, calf raises, step-downs, planks/side planks (2–3 sets each).

days.purpose: Build endurance volume while keeping overall week controlled under the current overreach signal.
days.target_intensity: Steady easy aerobic; finish feeling like you could continue.
days.terrain: Rolling is fine; keep climbs strictly aerobic.
days.title: Aerobic endurance ride (steady volume)
days.workout: 120 min aerobic Ride. Optional: 4 x 30s fast legs (not hard) with 4–5 min easy between if you feel fresh.

readiness.rationale: Deterministic readiness: steady (score 56.2). Authoritative readiness is steady, but overreach risk is moderate because your recent 7d training load is elevated versus your rolling baseline. With no recent sleep/RHR/HRV data in the last 7 days, we’ll keep intensity limited (1 quality day) and keep the week under a small ramp while maintaining frequency.

actions: Because last-7 sleep data are missing, treat recovery as a priority: aim to match your established 28-day sleep average (8.67 h) as closely as life allows.
Fuel the long trail day and the interval day: eat a carb-forward meal 2–3 h pre-session; during sessions >75–90 min, take steady carbs and fluids; include protein + carbs within ~60 min after.
Mobility: 10–15 min most days (calves/ankles, hips, glutes, thoracic spine). Keep it gentle, especially after the long trail day.
If legs feel beaten up from the prior week’s vertical, keep downhills conservative and consider swapping the 45 min run (2026-03-19) for an easy ride while keeping the strength session.

risks.message: Moderate overreach risk driven by elevated recent training load versus your rolling baseline; keep intensity limited to the single planned quality day and protect the rest day.
Recovery telemetry (sleep/RHR/HRV) is missing in the last 7 days, reducing confidence in day-to-day readiness; use subjective checks (motivation, soreness, irritability) to adjust down if needed.
Large recent vertical exposure from trail running suggests higher muscle damage risk; keep the long trail day easy and control descents.

snapshot.notes: Last 7 days were high-volume and high-load, driven by frequent rides plus one very vertical trail run. Sleep/RHR/HRV data are missing in the most recent 7-day window, so readiness is anchored primarily on the provided load-based forecast.
